name: Tradeoff-Bridge (MLLM/多模态)
full_intro_template: Paragraph 1 (Progress and promise): Multimodal foundation models have shown strong capabilities on <task family>. This progress suggests a promising path toward <broader capability>.

Paragraph 2 (Core tension): A central challenge in <target task> is the trade-off between <goal A> and <goal B>. Improving <A> often leads to degradation in <B>, especially under <difficult condition>.

Paragraph 3 (Why existing methods fail): Prior approaches typically focus on <single-side optimization>, and therefore cannot consistently handle both <A> and <B>. Moreover, there is still no systematic comparison across <settings>.

Paragraph 4 (Framework overview): We propose <framework name>, which first <step 1> to expose useful intermediate signals, and then <step 2> to suppress failure modes such as <hallucination / mismatch>.
Paragraph 5 (Efficiency component): To make the method practical, we further introduce <efficiency module>, reducing the computational overhead from <old cost> to <new cost> while preserving quality.
Paragraph 6 (Results): Extensive experiments on <benchmarks> show consistent improvements in both <metric for A> and <metric for B>. The gain is particularly strong in <hard subset>.
Paragraph 7 (Contributions list): Our contributions are three-fold: (i) <diagnosis>; (ii) <method>; (iii) <empirical validation + insight>.
abstract_template: P1: Define task and the A/B trade-off.
P2: Present framework and two-stage mechanism.
P3: Add efficiency or deployment detail.
P4: Report dual-metric improvements and scope.
sentence_stems: A key and open challenge is the mismatch between ... and ...
To address this issue, we propose a training-free framework ...
Extensive experiments demonstrate improvements on both ... and ... metrics.
